what_it_does: Records metadata for a single design-assets icon image stored as a blob, including its address, blob reference, dimensions, provenance, versioning, and links.
observations: The asset is an image/png with recorded width and height of 0
There is exactly one version (version 1) with the same blob and zero dimensions
The id and path contain parentheses and a space: 'icons/image (58)'
concerns: Width and height are 0, suggesting missing image analysis or ingestion metadata
Alt text is empty, which limits accessibility and searchability
The asset name 'image (58)' is generic and gives no semantic hint about the icon
conventions: YAML front-matter ledger record with id, address, channel, path, blob, mime, timestamps, version, versions, and links
URI-style identifiers for the asset (image://...) and its blob (blob://...)
Versioned asset history captured in a versions list
notes: A ledger entry for a generic icon asset with zero recorded dimensions and no alt text.
questions: Why are the width and height recorded as 0?
What visual content does this icon represent?
Is the empty alt text intentional or pending?
What does the 'dragnet' source indicate about how the asset was obtained?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to catalog a design-assets icon, but the actual record has zero dimensions and empty alt text, suggesting the catalog entry may be incomplete or awaiting analysis.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to register a captured icon image into a shared design-assets channel so it can be referenced, versioned, and linked by other records.
contribution: Provides a stable, addressable metadata record and blob pointer for an icon asset, enabling future linking, versioning, and retrieval within the design-assets channel.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML ledger record describing a design-assets icon image named 'image (58)' with a blob reference, zero recorded dimensions, dragnet provenance, and empty alt text.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and make addressable a single icon asset in the design-assets channel for reference and versioning.
